name: Triminator

consumes.description: Reads the active CompItem and its selectedLayers array. For each shape layer (matchName 'ADBE Vector Layer'), reads layer.selectedProperties to collect NAMED_GROUP properties; if none, targets the layer's 'ADBE Root Vectors Group'. Also reads ScriptUI.environment.keyboardState.shiftKey at startup to determine whether keyframes should be written.

functions: detect active composition and guard against missing comp or empty selection
skip non-shape layers (matchName !== 'ADBE Vector Layer') silently
collect selected NAMED_GROUP properties from each shape layer to determine target groups
add a Trim Paths property ('ADBE Vector Filter - Trim') to each target group's contents
when Shift key is not held, write keyframes on Trim Start and Trim End animating from 0% to 100% over the first second of the layer
wrap all mutations in a single undo group named 'Add Trim Paths'

scriptSim.scenario: success – add Trim Paths with keyframes to root vectors group
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Trim Comp' (1920×1080, 10 s, 24 fps); one Shape Layer selected ('Shape Layer 1', inPoint=0, outPoint=10); no shape groups selected; Shift key not held (addKeys=true)

operations.target: Shape Layer 1/Contents/ADBE Vector Filter - Trim/ADBE Vector Trim Start
Shape Layer 1/Contents/ADBE Vector Filter - Trim/ADBE Vector Trim Start
Shape Layer 1/Contents/ADBE Vector Filter - Trim/ADBE Vector Trim End
Shape Layer 1/Contents/ADBE Vector Filter - Trim/ADBE Vector Trim End

notes: HEADLESS: no ScriptUI dialog is shown; the script runs immediately when invoked.
Shift-key detection uses ScriptUI.environment.keyboardState.shiftKey evaluated at startup; holding Shift suppresses all keyframe generation.
Only layers whose matchName equals 'ADBE Vector Layer' (shape layers) are processed; all other layer types are silently skipped.

When no shape groups are selected, Trim Paths is applied to the layer's root vectors group ('ADBE Root Vectors Group'); when groups are selected only those groups' contents ('ADBE Vectors Group') are targeted.
Keyframes animate from 0% to 100% at times [layer.inPoint, layer.inPoint + 1] — a fixed 1-second ramp starting at the layer's in-point.
Both Trim Start and Trim End receive identical keyframe sets (0→100), creating a simultaneous reveal; downstream timing adjustment is expected.
The script is compatible with batch-selected layers and groups across a single pass.
